name: CurveBooleanRegions
summary: Represents the results of a Curve.CreateBooleanRegions calculation.

methods.name: BoundaryCount
methods.summary: Returns the number of boundary curves in a curve region.

returns.doc: The number of boundary curves in the curve region.

methods.name: Dispose()
methods.summary: Actively reclaims unmanaged resources that this instance uses.

methods.name: Dispose(Boolean)
methods.summary: This method is called with argument true when class user calls Dispose(), while with argument false when the Garbage Collector invokes the finalizer, or Finalize() method. You must reclaim all used unmanaged resources in both cases, and can use this chance to call Dispose on disposable fields if the argument is true. Also, you must call the base virtual method within your overriding method.

params.name: disposing
params.doc: True to release both managed and unmanaged resources; false to release only unmanaged resources

methods.name: PlanarCurve
methods.summary: Returns a planar curve that was calculated by Curve.CreateBooleanRegions.

returns.doc: The planar curve if successful, null if not successful.

methods.name: RegionCurves
methods.summary: Returns the boundary curves in a curve region. A curve region is a collection of curves that bound a single connected region of the plane. Note, the first curve is always the outer boundary.

returns.doc: An array of boundary curves if successful, an empty array if not successful.

methods.name: RegionPointIndex
methods.summary: If this object were created using the Curve.CreateBooleanRegions override that accepts a collection of points as input, then you this method to retrieve the index of the point contained in a curve region. If this.RegionPointIndex(i) = n, then points[i] is contained in this.RegionCurves(n). If points[i] is not in any region, then this.RegionPointIndex(i) = -1.

returns.doc: The index of the input point contained in the specified region if successful, or -1 if points[i] was not used in any region or if not successful.

methods.name: SegmentCount
methods.signature: public int SegmentCount(int regionIndex, int boundaryIndex)
methods.summary: Returns the number of segments in a boundary curve in a curve region.

returns.doc: The number of curve segments in th boundary curves.

methods.name: SegmentDetails
methods.signature: public int SegmentDetails(int regionIndex, int boundaryIndex, int segmmentIndex, out Interval subDomain, out bool reversed)
methods.summary: Returns the details of a segment in a boundary curve in a curve region.

params.name: subDomain
params.doc: The sub-domain of the planar curve used by the segment.

params.name: reversed
params.doc: true if the piece of the planar curve should be reversed.

returns.doc: The index of the planar curve used by the specified segment if successful, -1 if not successful.

properties.name: PlanarCurveCount
properties.summary: Returns number of planar curves that were calculated by Curve.CreateBooleanRegions.

properties.name: PointCount
properties.summary: If this object were created using the Curve.CreateBooleanRegions override that accepts a collection of points as input, then this value will be equal to the length of the points collection.

properties.name: RegionCount
properties.summary: Returns the number of curve regions. A curve region is a collection of curves that bound a single connected region of the plane.
